highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.203 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a login expiry warning so background sessions aren't interrupted mid-task — a helpful safeguard if you run long autonomous jobs. It also adds a grey pause badge in the footer when manual permission mode is active, making it easier to spot your current permission state at a glance.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.200 Released
highlights.summary: A significant behaviour change: Claude Code now defaults to Manual permission mode across CLI, VS Code, and JetBrains, meaning it will ask before taking actions rather than proceeding automatically. AskUserQuestion dialogs also no longer auto-continue by default — you can re-enable an idle timeout via /config if you prefer the old behaviour.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.202 Released
highlights.summary: You can now control the size of dynamic multi-agent workflows directly in /config with a new 'Dynamic workflow size' setting (small, medium, or large). Note that this is an advisory guideline rather than a hard cap, so Claude uses it to calibrate how many sub-agents to spin up rather than enforcing a strict limit.

highlights.title: The Making of Claude Code
highlights.summary: Anthropic's behind-the-scenes look at how Claude Code was designed and built, offering context on the product decisions and philosophy that shape the tool developers use every day. Understanding the design intent can help you work with Claude Code more effectively and anticipate how it will behave.

highlights.title: AI Coding Assistant Is Quietly Shipping Your Secrets
highlights.summary: A security-focused blog post warning that AI coding tools can inadvertently leak secrets such as API keys and credentials embedded in your codebase or context. Developers should audit what files and environment variables are accessible to Claude Code sessions and use .claudeignore or similar exclusions to limit exposure.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: A focused 30-minute YouTube tutorial aimed at getting developers productive with Claude Code quickly, covering core workflows and practical usage patterns. It's a good single-sitting watch if you want a structured overview without committing to a multi-hour course.

highlights.title: 9-Minute Claude Code Quickstart: Installation, Concepts & Real Usage (China-Accessible)
highlights.summary: A concise Bilibili tutorial that walks through installing Claude Code, explains how it works under the hood, and shows real-world usage — all in under 10 minutes and accessible from mainland China. Ideal for Chinese-speaking developers who want the fastest possible path from zero to a working setup.

highlights.title: AI Coding Is a Nightmare — Common Frustrations and How to Work Around Them
highlights.summary: A widely-upvoted Hacker News thread where developers share frustrations with AI coding assistants, including duplicate code generation and poor context window management. Reading through the discussion surfaces practical coping strategies — like providing explicit file references and breaking tasks into smaller chunks — that apply directly to Claude Code workflows.

tips_of_the_day: Create a .claudeignore file in your project root to explicitly exclude files containing secrets, API keys, or sensitive environment variables before starting any Claude Code session — treat it like a .gitignore for your AI assistant.

Now that Manual permission mode is the default in v2.1.200, use the grey pause badge in the VS Code footer as a quick sanity check before kicking off a long autonomous task — if you don't see it, verify your permission settings in /config.

When working on large tasks, use the new 'Dynamic workflow size' setting in /config to start with 'small' agent counts while you prototype, then scale up to 'medium' or 'large' once you're confident in the task definition — this keeps costs and context usage predictable.
